description: Shared deterministic task classification policy for the Python CLI and Node MCP server.

fanout.recommended_reason: Independent analysis or comparison work can be split across workers.
fanout.optional_reason: Use fanout only for independent subtasks; keep dependent implementation sequential.
fanout.not_recommended_reason: A single focused worker is simpler for this task type.

default.reason: Summary visibility is the default: show worker titles, status, and notable results without flooding the chat.

modes.reason: The prompt asks to keep background worker activity quiet.
The prompt asks for visible worker threads or separate chats when the host supports them.
The prompt asks to see detailed worker output or progress.

execution_profile.direct_reason: No protocol state is needed for a simple answer or one reversible edit.
execution_profile.full_reason: High-risk or heavy work needs the full plan, verify, reflect, and state loop.
execution_profile.ambiguous_reason: Ambiguous work needs a plan or fast triage before execution.
execution_profile.fast_reason: Focused low-risk work can skip plan state but must still use verify run.
execution_profile.light_reason: Light work can use the fast profile unless it expands into multiple steps.
execution_profile.standard_reason: Use a plan with verifiable steps and verify run before completion.

next_actions.direct: Answer directly or make the single reversible edit; no plan is required.
next_actions.fast: Use the fast profile: skip plan state, make the focused change, and run verify run before completion.
next_actions.standard: Create a plan with verifiable steps, act step by step, and use verify run before completion.
next_actions.full: Use the full loop: plan, memory, step updates, verify run, reflect on failures, and summarize.

model_triage.none_reason: The deterministic classifier is enough for a simple question or one-step task.
model_triage.high_impact_required_reason: High-impact ambiguous work deserves a cheap second read before planning.
model_triage.high_ambiguity_reason: The request is underspecified enough that a fast framing pass can reduce rework.
model_triage.recommended_reason: This problem type benefits from an independent framing pass before execution.
model_triage.optional_reason: A fast triage pass may help, but the main worker can proceed without it.
model_triage.skip_reason: The deterministic classification gives enough routing signal for this task.

verification_hints.security: Run security-focused tests plus the relevant normal suite; inspect permissions and secret handling.
verification_hints.release: Run full tests, package/build checks, and version or artifact checks before publishing.
verification_hints.migration: Run migration tests, compatibility checks, and rollback or fixture validation.
verification_hints.performance: Run targeted benchmarks or profiling before and after the change.
verification_hints.frontend_ui: Run build/lint plus browser or screenshot checks for affected views.
verification_hints.benchmark: Run the benchmark harness and record JSON output, pass rates, evidence rates, and durations.
verification_hints.research: Cite sources and record a verify claim only when no executable check exists.
verification_hints.review: Read diffs/files and report findings with file and line references; tests are supporting evidence.
verification_hints.debugging: Reproduce the failure first, then run the failing check again after the fix.
verification_hints.bugfix: Run the failing or targeted regression test, then the nearest broader suite.
verification_hints.test_generation: Run the added tests and confirm they fail before the fix when practical.
verification_hints.refactor: Run the existing test suite and any type, lint, or build checks.
verification_hints.feature: Run targeted tests for the feature plus the nearest broader suite.
verification_hints.docs: Run docs generation, link checks, or a text/build check when available.
verification_hints.design: Use verify claim for the design rationale, then create executable checks for implementation steps.
verification_hints.question: No executable check is required unless the answer makes a factual or time-sensitive claim.
verification_hints.trivial: Use the smallest available check, or no protocol command for a one-line answer.
